title: Deletion of Middle Node in Linked List

statement.markdown: Given a singly linked list where every node has a 'data' field to store some integer, you need to implement a method 'deleteMiddleElement' to delete a middle element in this linked list. The method should only take head of the linked list and the value of the middle node to be deleted as parameters. After deletion, the function should return head of the updated linked list.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be provided by the main function with head of the linked list and the value to be deleted.
statement.outputFormat: The function should print the head of the updated linked list and terminate execution.
statement.notes: Note that if there are multiple middle nodes with the same value, only the first one found should be deleted.

examples.explanation: In this example, the node with value 2 is deleted. The function should return head of the updated linked list.

functionSignature.python: def deleteMiddleElement(self, head, val: int) -> ListNode:
